title: Auditor Finding
description: One integrity flag emitted by an auditor skill. CRITICAL invariant: a finding with severity 'critical' or 'major' MUST carry at least one evidence entry with a non-empty span (enforced by tools/adjudicate_findings.py). A finding is NEVER a final verdict — the deterministic adjudicator computes the report verdict from the full finding set. The LLM reviewer proposes findings; it does not grade the paper.

pattern_id.description: Optional ref into references/hack-pattern-taxonomy.md (e.g. "HP-NUM-INFLATE"). The taxonomy is a post-hoc MAPPING layer, never the detector itself.

description.description: What was found, in reviewer-facing language. Describes a discrepancy/risk, NOT an accusation of misconduct.

observability_level_required.description: Lowest evidence tier at which this finding is decidable. The adjudicator downgrades severity if the run's observability level is below this (you cannot assert code-level fraud from a PDF).

evidence.description: Span-anchored evidence. Empty array is only permitted for severity 'info'.

claim_id.description: claim_id from the evidence ledger this finding is anchored to.

span.description: Verbatim quoted text the finding rests on.

location.description: Mirror of the ledger claim's location for human navigation.

artifact_hash.description: sha256 of the source artifact (from the ledger's evidence_anchor) — makes the finding reproducible.

verdict_local.description: This skill's LOCAL read. NOT the repo verdict. 'needs_external_check' = the claim (e.g. 'SOTA', 'first') cannot be settled inside this skill and is handed to baseline/citation forensics.

reviewer.description: Provenance of the cross-model judgment (reviewer-independence). Absent for purely deterministic findings produced by tools/.

deterministic.description: true when the finding came from a code rule (arithmetic, hash, regex), not an LLM.

false_positive_risk.description: Honest self-assessment — drives whether the finding is shown as a flag vs a note. Legit round numbers / single-seed pilots are common FPs.

recommended_reviewer_action.description: What a human reviewer should ASK or CHECK — never 'reject', always an investigative next step.
